briefing.headline: Cover water — post-spawn clear conditions reward moving baits that trigger reaction strikes.
briefing.gameplan:
In post-spawn with clear water and pre frontal conditions, moving baits presentations shine.

Wheeler: On lighter jigheads (1/8-3/16 oz) for Mooch Minnow swimbaits, cut off the tail tabs so the bait rolls more on the lighter head — gives more action for fish higher in the water column. On heavier heads (3/8-1/2 oz) keep the tail tabs intact as they won't affect action with the heavier weight

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, dock, point. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Use a heavier 3/4-ounce spinnerbait in windy conditions — easier to cast and the positive blade resistance makes it easier to feel bites than finesse presentations
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, dock, point. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Hackney: 'Fish a 6.8:1 reel with 16-lb fluorocarbon on a medium-heavy spinnerbait rod. The slower gear ratio helps maintain the medium to slow retrieve needed.'

KVD: 'Fall spinnerbaits: downsize to 3/16oz in clear water with small blades to match young-of-year baitfish. For heavy grass, go bigger with willowleaf blades. To fish fast and deep, put smaller blades on a 3/4oz head — the Scorcher design lets y...

Wheeler: Post-spawn offshore on Table Rock, the 3 key elements were brushpiles, isolated cedar trees, and bluff end points/big long main-lake points where schools related to current and hard bottom — mixed species (smallmouth, largemouth, spotted ...

briefing.depthStrategy: Pre-frontal post-spawn bass are transitioning and aggressive. Males guard fry in 2-6ft while females roam 5-12ft feeding heavily. Target both shallow fry-guarders and roaming post-spawn females near points.
adjustIf: If the front stalls and conditions stabilize, dial back aggression and focus on higher-percentage structure
If water clarity decreases from wind or rain, upsize lures and switch to brighter colors
If moving baits aren't getting bit, slow down to a crawling retrieve or switch to a jig
